title: pilosella bauhini subsp. viscidula
intro: Pilosella bauhini subsp. viscidula, also known as sticky mouse-ear, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as having antimicrobial properties that can help to combat infections. Pilosella bauhini subsp. viscidula is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, teas, and tinctures, and is also used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
